page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It features 30 vCPUs mapped to 15 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 59.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 1.97 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance and Geekbench text processing scoring in the top 25%. Small-model LLM inference is highly efficient, with text generation using a 135M model reaching top-tier performance in the top 10% at 314.72 tokens per second. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the average range. Passmark memory tests show strong performance, while cryptographic operations and compression tasks remain average.

Given the low memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance offers high resource density for compute-bound workloads without the added cost of excess memory. It is well-suited for CPU-intensive tasks such as software compilation, text processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference. The lack of local storage requires external block storage attachment, making it ideal for stateless compute nodes, batch processing, and distributed microservices.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-30 is a compute-optimized server instance featuring 30 vCPUs and 59.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 15 physical cores and x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier text generation speeds for small LLM models. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, making it a highly specialized option for compute-heavy workloads. The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1.97 GB per core provides a cost-efficient profile for tasks that demand raw processing power over large memory footprints. It is highly suitable for software compilation, text processing, batch computing, and stateless web serving.

tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 server featuring thirty vCPUs and AMD EPYC processing, designed for high-density CPU workloads and text processing.

Equipped with 59.0 GB of RAM, optimized for compute-heavy tasks
Strong single-core CPU performance and top-tier small-model LLM text generation
